Fixed and verified end-to-end on Bazzite/KDE Wayland: wl-paste watcher captures live copies into the DB; KDE shortcut registered and activated via kglobalaccel D-Bus without re-login.

Root cause found: Electron 42 regression on KDE Wayland (Bazzite) — no window is ever composited under the native Wayland backend, and under forced x11 the GPU process crash-loops and no X11 windows are created. Verified with a minimal red-window repro script: invisible on 42, visible on 33. Fixed by pinning Electron back to ^33 (runs via XWayland; windows, positioning, and cursor queries all work). Verified popup visibly on screen via screenshot.

Popup refresh verified live on the host: clip count updated from stale 19 to 30 on show, marker string captured post-startup appears in DB and list. Clear History wired via clips:clear IPC + native dialog; clearAllClips covered by unit tests (84 passing).

KWin helper script: focus-restore before paste injection (works for Wayland-native targets like VSCode), true-cursor popup placement, center-on-cursor-screen. Verified on host: popup maps at true cursor position while X11 pointer is stale elsewhere.

Implemented via AT-SPI with dbus-next. Verified on host: popup maps 12px below Konsole's live caret with Qt Wayland origin fix-up. Chromium apps emit after next launch (toolkit-accessibility enabled). Falls back to mouse placement whenever the caret is unknown or untrustworthy.
Verified on host via popup screenshot: HTML clips display tag-stripped text with HTML badge; caret code fully removed, config migrates to mouse automatically, gsettings flag reverted.

Dual-flavor capture and paste verified on host: content_text stored for dual-flavor sources, NULL + naive-strip fallback for html-only sources, clipboard.write presents both flavors. Save HTML toggle remains the kill-switch.
Verified live on host: copying A, B, A-again leaves a single A row at the top of history. htmlToText covered by unit tests (92 passing) including multi-line digest case.

resetDedup() wired into clips:clear and clips:delete handlers; capture verified working on the deployed build. Dialog-click flow left for user verification.

Plain-paste modifier verified end-to-end via ydotool selection (plain-only flavors with Ctrl, both flavors without). Dialog centering follows the proven popup re-assert pattern; visual confirmation on next Settings open.

Windows paste via PowerShell SendKeys implemented (macOS still TODO, left noted in paste.ts). Runtime verification pending user's Windows machine.

AppImage built and verified on host: double-click executable runs, captures, opens popup via --show-popup second-instance, and registers the KDE shortcut with the stable $APPIMAGE path. CI publishes it per release.

Shipped in v0.1.0: NSIS one-click per-user installer/uninstaller for Windows; AppImage/deb for Linux with first-run ensureYdotoold() self-setup and 'ctrlc --teardown' uninstall (removes DE shortcut, autostart, ydotoold unit, KWin helper; keeps history). CI release workflow publishes all artifacts on tags.

Packaged binary is named ctrlc / CtrlC.exe via electron-builder executableName+productName — task managers show CtrlC instead of electron. Verified on Linux (pgrep shows ctrlc processes for the AppImage).
Verified by rendering dist/renderer/about.html in a 380x440 useContentSize window: zero overflow, logo displays above Serious Geese, LLC, screenshot inspected.
Verified via Electron renderer harness on the built popup: Del deletes selected row, selection lands on next item, context menu opens, Edit round-trips through clips:update. 94 unit tests pass.
